strengths: Built and deployed production-grade agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators that enable scalable agent workflows — directly aligning with the need for tool-augmented, stateful agents beyond prompt chaining.

Proven track record in high-scale distributed systems: designed low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec using DLang and async I/O, demonstrating deep backend and event-driven architecture expertise required for real-time fintech inference.

Demonstrated leadership in scaling engineering culture through TDD, CI/CD, pair programming, and internal workshops — including founding open-source tools and leading dev-ops infrastructure, mirroring the need for disciplined, high-leverage team growth and observability focus.
concerns: No explicit mention of fintech infrastructure experience (credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance) — critical domain knowledge for auditable AI decisioning in finance.
Lack of documented experience with multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph or production-grade LLM evaluation pipelines — key technical requirements for the role’s agentic AI architecture mandate.
Current role at re:cinq is recent (April 2025); insufficient time to demonstrate full ownership of architectural decisions or team scaling beyond individual contributor contributions.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, demonstrable production experience building agentic AI infrastructure and high-scale distributed systems — directly matching the core technical mandates of the CTO role — while showing consistent leadership in engineering discipline, tooling, and culture; his lack of fintech domain exposure is a gap, but his proven ability to architect complex, real-time, auditable systems in high-stakes environments suggests strong transferability with targeted onboarding.
